string_constants(3) Library Functions Manual string_constants(3)

NAME

string_constants - String Literal Constants


- Common string literal constants for boolean and value comparisons.

SYNOPSIS

Files


file string_constants.h
String literal constants.

Macros


#define STR_ZERO '0'
String literal: 0"(zero). #define STR_ONE '1'
String literal: 1"(one). #define STR_FALSE 'false'
String literal: false". #define STR_TRUE 'true'
String literal: true". #define STR_OFF 'off'
String literal: off". #define STR_ON 'on'
String literal: on". #define STR_NO 'no'
String literal: no". #define STR_YES 'yes'
String literal: yes".

Detailed Description

Common string literal constants for boolean and value comparisons.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define STR_FALSE 'false'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "false". Used for boolean string comparisons.

#define STR_NO 'no'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "no". Used for boolean string comparisons.

#define STR_OFF 'off'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "off". Used for boolean string comparisons (disable/enable).

Definition at line 58 of file string_constants.h.

#define STR_ON 'on'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "on". Used for boolean string comparisons (enable/disable).

Definition at line 67 of file string_constants.h.

#define STR_ONE '1'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "1" (one). Used for boolean comparisons and numeric string parsing.

#define STR_TRUE 'true'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "true". Used for boolean string comparisons.

#define STR_YES 'yes'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "yes". Used for boolean string comparisons.

Definition at line 85 of file string_constants.h.

#define STR_ZERO '0'

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/string_constants.h>

String literal: "0" (zero). Used for boolean comparisons and numeric string parsing.
